summary: User policy for the Windows Spotlight welcome experience. Good for a quieter onboarding flow, but it removes one of the post-update or first-run introduction surfaces in Windows.

setting.name: Windows Spotlight Welcome Experience
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can show a Spotlight-based welcome or introductory experience after updates or sign-in. This policy decides whether that welcome experience is allowed for the current user.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it to reduce interruptions after updates, simplify onboarding, or keep the interface free from promotional welcome screens.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off reduces extra welcome surfaces, but it also removes guided introduction screens that can explain new Windows features.
setting.professional_notes: The Experience Policy CSP uses the allow-form name AllowWindowsSpotlightWindowsWelcomeExperience, while CloudContent.admx maps the user Group Policy to DisableWindowsSpotlightWindowsWelcomeExperience with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not force the welcome-experience policy for the current user.

allowed_values.label: Welcome experience allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and Spotlight welcome experiences remain available.

allowed_values.label: Welcome experience disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Spotlight welcome experiences are blocked by policy.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DisableWindowsSpotlightWindowsWelcomeExperience = 1, which matches the documented Group Policy disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows user-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 10 version 1703 and newer supported systems

states.rationale: The user policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: The Experience Policy CSP documents the allow-form default as allowed, while the registry policy baseline remains not configured unless the policy is set.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: People who want Windows intro and welcome guidance
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep its normal welcome experience behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a cleaner post-update and sign-in flow.
tradeoffs: Windows can continue showing welcome and intro experiences.
No policy-based restriction is enforced for this surface.

recommended_profiles.label: Quiet UI
avoid_for: Users who want guidance about new Windows features

states.rationale: Turn off Spotlight welcome experiences through Group Policy.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want fewer post-update and introductory Windows surfaces.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want Windows to explain new features through welcome screens.

evidence.title: Microsoft Policy CSP: AllowWindowsSpotlightWindowsWelcomeExperience
evidence.summary: Official documentation for the allow-form policy controlling the Windows Spotlight welcome experience.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft CloudContent.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps the user Group Policy to DisableWindowsSpotlightWindowsWelcomeExperience with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0 under the CloudContent policy key.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DisableWindowsSpotlightWindowsWelcomeExperience = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: Local Microsoft CloudContent policy files document the exact user-policy surface, numeric mapping, and welcome-experience behavior. The app writes the documented disabled value.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: <policy name="DisableWindowsSpotlightWindowsWelcomeExperience" class="User" ... key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CloudContent" valueName="DisableWindowsSpotlightWindowsWelcomeExperience">; CloudContent.adml: If you enable this policy, the Windows Welcome Experience will no longer display when there are updates and changes to Windows and its apps.
validation_proof.notes: Local official ADMX defines the exact user-policy key and value name; local ADML confirms that enabling the policy suppresses the Windows Welcome Experience.
